d9edef4fa5a7e12a.title: What I Learned from Pre-training a 126M Parameter LLM from Scratch
d9edef4fa5a7e12a.query: A deeply technical personal blog post explaining a novel trick for LLM pretraining at scale, written by a practitioner rather than a company blog.
d9edef4fa5a7e12a.why: This post is a basic replication of Andrej Karpathy's nanoGPT/nanochat tutorial without introducing any novel engineering insights or deep technical breakthroughs.

ed885d5a4aa9016e.title: WarpGroup: Optimizing LLM Training by GPU Optimization with a Custom C++ Backend | Medium
ed885d5a4aa9016e.query: A deeply technical personal blog post explaining a novel trick for LLM pretraining at scale, written by a practitioner rather than a company blog.
ed885d5a4aa9016e.why: A highly technical, hands-on engineering post detailing a custom C++ backend for PyTorch to optimize variable-length sequence batching with concrete performance numbers.
points: Replaces standard zero-padding in LLM batching with a custom C++ backend implementing VRAM-aware bin packing for variable-length sequences.
Achieves a 2.08x throughput speedup on NVIDIA H100 and a 5.89x speedup on GTX 1080 while eliminating out-of-memory (OOM) crashes.
Implements pinned-memory transfers to optimize host-to-device data movement.

0359941d815d193d.title: I Built a Complete LLM From Scratch in Pure Rust — Pretraining, GRPO, Flash Attention, and Self-Learning Without a Single Line of Python | by Aarambh Dev Hub | Jun, 2026 | Medium
0359941d815d193d.query: A deeply technical personal blog post explaining a novel trick for LLM pretraining at scale, written by a practitioner rather than a company blog.
0359941d815d193d.why: A highly technical project implementing a complete LLM pipeline in pure Rust, though building LLMs from scratch in Rust is becoming a more common educational genre.
points: Implements a complete LLM pipeline (pretraining, GRPO, Flash Attention) entirely in Rust using the Candle tensor framework.
Eliminates Python, PyTorch, and C++ wrappers, achieving a pure Rust implementation of modern LLM training and inference.

6fc2cbeba98b0293.title: Sidon Pretraining Costs Zero Perplexity: First Experimental Results | by Micheal Bee | May, 2026 | Medium
6fc2cbeba98b0293.query: A deeply technical personal blog post explaining a novel trick for LLM pretraining at scale, written by a practitioner rather than a company blog.
6fc2cbeba98b0293.why: Highly niche, original research exploring a novel mathematical constraint (Sidon sets) on embedding pretraining to achieve lossless compressed attention, backed by experimental validation.
points: Proposes a soft Sidon constraint on embedding pretraining to keep pair-sums and k-tuple-sums distinct in a small subspace.
Aims to make 128-to-1 compressed attention lossless by combining Sidon geometry with DeepSeek-style positional bias.
Evaluates the mathematical proposal using NanoGPT validation runs on the Tiny Shakespeare dataset.

0657fbe29c8a0500.title: 'Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max | Sean Weldon
0657fbe29c8a0500.query: A deeply technical personal blog post explaining a novel trick for LLM pretraining at scale, written by a practitioner rather than a company blog.
0657fbe29c8a0500.why: A high-quality technical synthesis of advanced memory optimization techniques required to scale transformer context lengths to millions of tokens.
points: Details the orchestration of FSDP, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, and sequence length tiling to train 5M token contexts.
Explains how to overcome the quadratic memory growth of self-attention matrices (e.g., 3M x 3M interaction matrices) on an 8x H100 node.
Introduces a novel optimization termed 'Untitled Ulysses' to maintain competitive performance at 8B and 32B parameter scales.

33e4677b031b2030.why: A solid, well-structured technical guide comparing modern preference alignment algorithms with mathematical objectives and VRAM reduction details.
points: Compares the mathematical objectives of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) and Kahneman-Tversky Optimization (KTO) for reward-model-free alignment.
Explains how Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) reduces training memory by over 50% by eliminating the critic model.
Provides a Python simulation for modeling GRPO loss to help understand its mechanics.

points: Focuses on the undocumented production failure modes and practical engineering challenges of implementing Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).

7278dd3a74ace632.title: CUDA Kernels and FlashAttention: Why Memory Bandwidth Is the Bottleneck | Amulya Bhatia
7278dd3a74ace632.query: A niche blog post explaining GPU kernel internals or memory layout tricks for transformer inference, written for someone who already knows the basics.
7278dd3a74ace632.why: A very clear, mathematically grounded explanation of CUDA kernels and memory bottlenecks in LLM inference, though the concepts of FlashAttention and kernel fusion are somewhat well-known.
points: Explains how GPU memory bandwidth, rather than compute capacity, acts as the primary bottleneck during LLM inference.
Grounded in concrete numbers using Gemma 4 12B as a running example to illustrate memory-bound operations.
Details how custom kernels in frameworks like vLLM and SGLang achieve 2-4x throughput improvements over standard PyTorch kernels.

7f5d996f70ddce97.title: Learning FlashAttention the Hard Way — Part 2 — RiftStack
7f5d996f70ddce97.query: A niche blog post explaining GPU kernel internals or memory layout tricks for transformer inference, written for someone who already knows the basics.
7f5d996f70ddce97.why: An exceptional, deep-dive technical post that reproduces FlashAttention-2 on an RTX 5090 using an ML compiler, showing how an autotuner matches hand-written SOTA kernels to the microsecond.
points: Demonstrates how the Emmy compiler's autotuner can automatically discover the exact thread/block geometry of hand-written FlashAttention-2 kernels.
Evaluates and quantifies transferable optimizations from FlashAttention-3 and FlashAttention-4 on consumer hardware (RTX 5090).
Explains the lowering of associative reductions to GPU hardware, including register-tiling and shared memory operand staging.
